title: From GST to tariffs, for insurance sector, it’s been a year of reset, realignment and renewed focus
author: ET CONTRIBUTORS
short_desc: India's insurance sector transformed in 2025. GST changes made life and health insurance more affordable. Digital tools improved access and service. Stricter regulations built trust. Insurers and policyholders now share responsibilities. This year set the stage for a stronger, consumer-focused insur
long_desc: As calendar year 2025 comes to a close, the Indian insurance sector stands at a transformative juncture. This year has brought sweeping changes, from GST reforms and regulatory tightening to distribution recalibrations and accelerated digital adoption, reshaping the way insurers operate and how consumers experience insurance. These developments have implications beyond commercial metrics; they touch trust, affordability, and the protection of millions of policyholders across the country.Among the most impactful changes was the removal of GST on life and health insurance premiums, effective September, making coverage more accessible for households, particularly in the missing-middle segment. Families are expected to find it easier to secure and maintain protection, while new sections of…

title: FACSI urges Centre to ease GST, credit norms for MSEs in 2026-27 Union Budget
author: PTI
short_desc: The Federation of Associations of Cottage and Small Industries is urging the government to prioritize substantial backing for micro and small enterprises in the next Union Budget. They are pushing for critical measures such as tax relief, enhanced credit facilities, the formation of a dedicated advi
long_desc: Kolkata, The Federation of Associations of Cottage and Small Industries (FACSI) has urged the Centre for tax, credit and regulatory relief measures for micro and small enterprises in the 2026-27 Union Budget, to sustain growth and strengthen their role in the industrial ecosystem.In a recent pre-budget letter to Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, FACSI president H K Guha said the recommendations were framed after consultations with various associations of entrepreneurs and MSE groups across the country.Among the key demands, the industry body has sought the constitution of an exclusive council for small and micro enterprises under the Ministry of MSME, a higher exemption threshold under the GST regime and a single, simplified GST return for small units.FACSI has also called for…
